scenario_results.reason: The lane advanced a routine studio day only when the call sheet, stage booking, permit packet, audience and talent records, and wrap artifacts aligned.
Missing production, stage, lifecycle, permit, and release prerequisites produced a blocked-state response instead of speculative studio support.
The lane refused to mark work complete when daily production reporting, time-report, waiver, or wrap evidence was incomplete.
Conflicting booking, permit, and facilities signals triggered rework or escalation rather than unsupported venue closure.
Unsigned release and likeness-permission gaps were routed to legal-rights ownership rather than cleared in-lane.
Minor-participant handling stayed blocked until the required labor evidence and owner routing were established.
Higher-risk soundstage work was escalated to safety and specialty owners instead of being treated as ordinary studio coordination.
Schedule and labor dependency conflicts were kept in exception handling and not silently reconciled by the lane.
The in-scope scenario preserved studio-specific terminology such as call sheet, one-line schedule, holding, greenroom, Exhibit G, and wrap rather than generic event wording.
Generic media-event framing was rejected until production-specific studio records and workflow context were named.
Conflicting venue, permit, and tenant-policy directions triggered escalation rather than false-confidence synthesis.
Tenant adaptation preserved distinct audience, talent, and studio control paths instead of flattening them into one generic workflow.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required abilities are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with complete ability coverage for the execution-processing-and-fulfillment and cross-industry contract.

The package stays inside studio operations and does not claim legal rights clearance, labor interpretation, payroll approval, production-management signoff, engineering, or fire-life-safety authority.
